chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: So we have seen a lot of things about Python programming  Today I will tell you how to handle a file in Python  Because file handling is a very important concept in Python  How to open a file, how to write it, how to append it in that text file  That text file can also be a binary file

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: How to handle it in that case, I will tell you all this in today's video  Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started  So the file handling methods in Python are built in  In this example, I will make a Python file first  So that it is very easy for you to understand

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: Then we will see how to open a basic file in Python  When we talk about a file, the first thing that comes to our mind is a text file  Although files can be text files or binary files  And there can be many types of files in text files  In which textual content can be there

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: But for now, let's see in a very simple way how file handling is done in Python  So Python provides several ways to manipulate files  Today we are going to discuss that  To open a file, you can use the open method  So simply you can say f is equal to open myfile.txt

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: Now this myfile.txt is not present here, it is not present at all  If I run it, I will see the error only  Now you see here it is saying that what is myfile.txt  How to open myfile.txt  How will it open if there is no file

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: So this is what is being said by Python  Now you see here that Python provides the open function to open a file  It takes two arguments  The first argument is the name of the file  And the second argument is the mode in which the file should be opened

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: The mode can be R for reading, W for writing and A for appending  That means you can read a file, you can write it with W  And you can append in that file if you want  That means you can add additional content in that file at the end of the file  You can do that with append mode by writing A here

chunks.title: File IO in Python
chunks.text: All these things will continue  Now I will tell you one more thing  That if I want to open this file, I can make it  So I do one thing here, I make a new file  I will name it myfile.txt

I have extracted the content of this text file  If I had opened it in write mode, then I would have been able to extract the content of this file
